title: Weekly Market Report | Jun 29 - Jul 3, 2026
report:
# Weekly Market Report | June 29 - July 3, 2026

---

## Japan Market Overview

The Nikkei rebounded modestly. Three straight gains on AI/semiconductor dip-buying early in the week gave way to a sharp pullback after the U.S. SOX index tumbled -- still a rough ride around 70,000. The week's defining feature, however, was *breadth*. Even on 7/2, when the index fell 1,741 points, roughly 80% of TSE Prime names rose and TOPIX extended gains. The Nikkei/TOPIX divergence shows capital is not leaving Japan -- it is rotating out of AI mono-focus into everything else.

**Nikkei 225 Weekly Path**
- Mon 6/29: 69,468 (+107). Modest rebound. U.S.-Iran mutual ceasefire agreed, Bahrain talks set. Growth 250 jumped +2.96%
- Tue 6/30: 70,062 (+594). Reclaimed 70,000 on the prior day's +3.8% SOX rally -- but 64% of names declined, an index-heavy advance
- Wed 7/1: 70,474 (+412). Third straight gain. BOJ Tankan hit an 8-year high. An early +1,900 surge faded
- Thu 7/2: 68,733 (-1,741 / -2.47%). SOX crashed -6.27% overnight; AI/semis fell across the board. Yet TOPIX rose with ~80% of Prime advancing
- Fri 7/3: 69,744 (+1,010 / +1.47%). Weak U.S. payrolls cooled rate-hike expectations; Dow's record close provided the tailwind

**Weekly Change: 69,360 -> 69,744, +383 (+0.55%)**

**Reading Friday's tape**: Chicago futures pointed to 68,325, yet the cash market surged +1,010. Weak payrolls flipped into "bad news is good news" as rate-hike odds fell -- and the leaders were autos, banks, and domestic demand, not semis. Friday's strength stress-tested the new market structure and it held. Caveat: the U.S. is closed 7/3, so Tokyo heads into ETF distribution-selling week with little overseas guidance.

---

## Strongest Themes & Stocks

**#1 Cyber Security: promoted to national-policy status**
The Five Eyes emergency advisory on 7/2 sent FFRI Security (3692) +8.7%, HENNGE (4475) +7.9%, and Cybozu (4776) +8.2%. Geopolitical risk migrating into cyberspace will not fade quickly; as a low-index-correlation policy theme, this can be a medium-term portfolio pillar.

**#2 Banking & Finance: laggard catch-up**
Rate-hike beneficiaries like Tsukuba Bank (8338, +8.5%) drew inflows, starring in Thursday's "index down, stocks up" session. The weak payrolls may slow the pace, but the catch-up rotation itself should persist.

**#3 Power Semiconductors & Energy Storage: the AI power bottleneck trade**
Sanken Electric (6707) hit limit-up (+12.85%) on strong results and a dividend hike on 6/30. Birdman (7063) went limit-up on grid-scale battery operations. AI datacenter power constraints feed a long-duration theme -- generation, transmission, storage, power semis -- that gets bought even when AI corrects.

**Also notable**: VTuber pair Cover (5253) +8.5% and Anycolor (5032) +7.8% lifted Gaming & Entertainment; Visional (4194) +8.1% led a re-rating of profitable SaaS. Growth 250's +2.96% Monday marks a clear bottoming attempt in small caps.

## Weakest Themes & Stocks

**Memory: Kioxia (285A) broke its 25-day line**
A clear warning from this year's leader. Advantest (6857), Tokyo Electron (8035), and Ibiden (4062) also slid on 7/2. As Japan's largest company by market cap, Kioxia's fight at the 25-day line is the thermometer for the memory trade -- and for the Nikkei itself. A failure to recapture it means a longer correction, though large Korean capex reports offer dip-buying support for equipment names.

**Also weak**: OKI (6703) -7.2% and Zojirushi (7965) -7.0% on news-exhaustion selling.

---

## U.S. Market

Early in the week the Dow notched consecutive records with SOX up ~4%; the tone flipped when SOX crashed -6.27% on 7/1. On 7/2, payrolls came in at +57k vs +113k expected -- rate-hike odds fell, the Dow closed at a record 52,900 (+594), while storage names SanDisk, Seagate, and Western Digital dragged the Nasdaq lower. The "Dow up, Nasdaq down" reversal mirrors Tokyo's value rotation. U.S. markets closed 7/3 for Independence Day.

---

## Key Economic Data

- BOJ Tankan (June): Large manufacturers' DI +22 (consensus +16; highest in 8 years 3 months)
- U.S. Nonfarm Payrolls (June): +57k (far below +113k consensus)
- USD/JPY: modest yen strength into the low-161s (161.83 -> ~161.43)

---

## What to Watch Next Week (7/6-7/10)

1. **ETF distribution selling (around 7/8-7/10)**: ~1 trillion yen of mechanical cash-raising sales. The week's biggest supply-demand event -- historically, the post-selling rebound has been a prime dip-buying window
2. **Options SQ (7/10)**: SQ week in a volatile tape invites positioning-driven swings
3. **Kioxia (285A) vs. its 25-day line**: recapture = index rebound; failure = extended correction
4. **Apr-Jun earnings previews**: early reporters like Yaskawa Electric (6506) offer a first read on manufacturing and China demand ahead of the late-July earnings flood
5. **Themes & stocks to watch**: cyber security follow-through (FFRI 3692, Digital Arts 2326); Korean mega-capex plays in equipment (Kokusai Electric 7729) and PCBs (Meiko 6787, Ibiden 4062); consumption-tax-cut food names (Kobe Bussan 3038, Zensho 7550); power semis (Sanken 6707, Rohm 6963)
6. **FX**: yen strength toward low-161s. Not yet painful for exporters, but acceleration would undercut the auto-led value trade

---

## StockWaveJP Editorial View

Beneath the index turbulence, a structural shift from AI mono-focus to earnings- and value-driven breadth is underway. The 8-year-high Tankan lays groundwork for the earnings rally to spread beyond AI. With ETF distribution selling looming, we favor buying dips in tranches. Our two-step playbook: (1) build positions in low-correlation policy/domestic themes -- cybersecurity, rare earths, food -- now; (2) wait for Kioxia to recapture its 25-day line before adding back to AI/semiconductor dips.

*This report is for informational purposes only and does not constitute investment advice. All investment decisions are your sole responsibility.*
